Şimdi, uluslararası hukuka göre kıta sahanlığı ana kıtalar, ana karalar üzerinden yapılır ama bu Sevilla haritasına göre Yunanistan: "Meis Adası benimdir ve Meis Adası üzerinde kıta sahanlığı yapacağım." diyor arkadaşlar; bize 2 kilometre, Yunanistan ana karasına 580 kilometre ama burada kıta sahanlığını ortaya koyuyor.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Tamamlayın sözlerinizi.
ERHAN USTA (Devamla) - Bitiriyorum Sayın Başkan.
Hâlbuki uluslararası başka örnekler var; İngiltere ve Fransa tahkim kararı var; bakın aynı. Orada ana kıtalar üzerinden yapılmış eşit mesafe yöntemine göre, orta noktayı bulmuşlar, kıta sahanlıklarını ona göre ayırmışlar. Yemen-Eritre kararı var. Bütün uluslararası mahkeme kararları bizim lehimize olmasına rağmen biz şunu bırak kabul ettirmeyi karşı tarafa iddia dahi edemiyoruz, Meis Adası üzerinden bir kıta sahanlığı yapılıyor. Dolayısıyla, tabii, bu şekildeki bir dış politikanın getirdiği sonuç da fakirlik olacaktır.
Ben sözlerimi daha fazla uzatmak istemiyorum, çok teşekkür ediyorum.
Dolayısıyla, milleti tüketen iktidarın biz bu tükeniş bütçesine İYİ Parti Grubu olarak "hayır" oyu kullanacağımızı ifade ediyor, Genel Kurulu saygıyla selamlıyorum. (İYİ Parti ve YENİ YOL sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) ON BEHALF OF IYI PARTY GROUP - Dear MPs,
The great Turkish nation watching us on their screens; I greet you all with respect.
Today, we are on the last day of the two-month budget marathon. In this speech, I will try to focus on more structural and general issues. Because for the last sixty days, for about two months, we made technical analysis, showed graphs, well, we gave figures both in the Planning and Budget Commission and in the General Assembly. Now, thinking that it would be more useful to delve into the root causes of the problems we are experiencing, I will share my evaluations with you on that subject. Of course, these also stand out as areas we need to improve. Now, of course, these areas, in my opinion, are the root causes of the poverty and troubles, especially the economic troubles, that Türkiye is experiencing today, directly or indirectly.
Now, the first issue, dear friends, is that the Turkish economy does not produce enough, that is, unfortunately, the country's production capacity is weak. The share of the manufacturing industry in national income. When we look at good example countries, European countries or countries in the Far East such as South Korea and China, the share of the manufacturing industry in national income is extremely low in our country because we do not allocate enough resources to productive areas. What's the reason for this? The reason for this is this rent economy, the concrete economy, that emerged especially during the AK Party governments. Look, for twenty-three years, Türkiye has used approximately 677 billion dollars in outsourcing from abroad, and we also have our own resources. For God's sake, take a look and see, has Türkiye's production capacity increased this much? At least. What is outsourcing? Investment is the difference between savings. In other words, we are talking about the money we use from outside to finance our investments in places where our own savings are not enough, but when you look at it, we see that Türkiye's investment capacity has not increased that much. Where did this money go? It went to concrete plus it went to corruption.
I claim this very clearly: Money that goes out of Türkiye unregistered is also included in these figures. Now, what needed to be done for this? Urban rents had to be taxed. This issue has been studied, but in this regard - these studies have been completed for almost ten years, we worked on them while we were in the state, but these studies are not concluded in any way because there is a government and its supporters who benefit from this.
Now, what's our other problem with production? The technology level of production is low. Of course, we accept this: In other words, during the AK PARTY governments period, there was a transition from the lower, or rather low, technology level to the medium technology level, but we could not achieve the transition from medium technology to high technology, and even when we compare it with 2002, we see that the share of high technology products in production and exports has decreased. How does this pose a problem to us right now? Currently, especially in labor-intensive sectors, companies either close down because they cannot compete or go abroad to countries where labor is cheaper.

Türkiye has made the following mistake so far, during the AK Party governments' period: It chose to compete on labor, or more precisely, on wages. However, we needed to produce higher technology, higher value-added products; Unfortunately, this could not be achieved, not enough resources were allocated here.
Another problem is regarding production, our country's productivity level is very low, dear friends. It is low both in terms of labor productivity and in terms of total factor productivity in the economy as a whole. What's in this? There is company scale, there is the legal system, there is the justice system, there is the business environment, there are uncertainties, there is the deterioration of expectations in the economy, there is property security. The deteriorations here appear as inefficiency in the overall economy, which reduces Türkiye's competitiveness.
In terms of goods and services - I continue regarding production - Türkiye's dependence on abroad has increased. However, in a twenty-three-year period of power, in a long period of power - this dependence existed in the past, we accept it - but this dependence was
It did not decrease, but increased both in terms of financing and goods. Without many high-tech products, we cannot invest in Türkiye, we cannot produce, or we have to import some raw materials, although some of these could have been corrected until today.
Now, we see that the problems of exporters and industrialists have increased significantly recently, and we have been warning about this for a year. Look, we said that exporters and industrialists will experience more of the problems experienced by tradesmen, farmers, civil servants and retirees. All ears were blocked, and now we see this at this stage. The ratio of exports to national income - look, legends have been written about exports, right - says that it will drop to 2002 levels. The 2028 MTP, friends, means that legend is over, the talisman is broken, exports are in a really difficult situation, industrialists are in a really difficult situation.
Now, such an economic program is being implemented that "We will reduce demand." they said. It turned into an economic program that curtailed production, hindered production, and punished production; We expressed and asked about the details of this in our previous budget speeches. That's why we asked the Minister of Finance the following in our speeches: "Will you implement practices that will strengthen the economic program you have implemented and expand its scope?" We said, "Why don't you take steps to tax urban rents? What will you do for this?" We asked both Cevdet Yılmaz, the Minister of Finance and the Minister of Agriculture. Sir, "In order to further increase production in agriculture, the resources allocated to agricultural support need to be increased. Will you increase this?" We asked, but we couldn't get a positive answer from any of them.
Now, the second problem area, friends, is that when production is low, of course, the country's business and employment capacity is also insufficient, and there is a need to increase it here too. Approximately 500 thousand people enter the workforce of working age every year. We have to find jobs for our people, we have to train the people the market demands. There is a need for comprehensive education-employment planning, unfortunately there is a skill mismatch. "Between the people the employment market demands and the people coming out of our education systems".

"(*) there is, there is an incompatibility, it needs to be seen.
Of course, in order to be a business environment, there must be growth. Our growth is not bad, we have an average growth rate of 5 percent, although it has decreased recently, but this is not a high growth rate that will actually meet Türkiye's needs. In the past, we had made plans and programs targeting 7 percent growth, but now the growth has been around for a long time, look, the growth in the last four years - with the projections - will be around 3 percent. This growth will further increase employment difficulties.
The demographic window of opportunity is closing, friends; It was our last chance, unfortunately, in twenty-two, twenty-three years, AK PARTY ended Türkiye's chance. I have previously explained what this is; Now, I can't explain the details because I don't have time.
Look, only 2/3 of the working age population among men works, and only 1/3 among women. It is not possible for such an economy to reach high income levels. All the figures given for high income levels are wrong. I claim that we have proven these. To date, no one has been able to come up with a logical or technical explanation against our evidence or these claims. The idle workforce rate reached 30 percent. This has now become ossified here, this is a very dangerous situation. Young people want to leave the country, both the educated and the uneducated want to leave.
Dear friends, the third structural problem area is that Türkiye does not have a population strategy, it does not have a population policy. Can such a large country have an economy that has no population policy and leaves its population haphazardly to its own devices? Well, we just talked about the employment problems. As a continuation of this, the fertility rate in Türkiye is falling sharply. 1.48 children, the figure for 2024. An even bigger problem is that there is a huge difference in regional distribution, that is, we also have provinces where the fertility rate is 3.28; We also have provinces where it is 1.12. There is no policy, no action regarding this. Now we ask the Minister of Family, we say: Dear Minister, in order to increase the fertility rate, the President said "Have 3 children." What policy do you implement other than your heroism? any
We cannot encounter a policy. Of course, you will not have the opportunity to change the course of things for which you do not allocate resources.
We talked about the issues regarding nurseries and said that nurseries should be built. We said that these opportunities and infrastructure should be prepared so that women can both participate in the workforce and have children. No action is taken regarding this.
We have empty villages, we have uncultivated lands. For example, we asked the Minister of Internal Affairs - that is, our own citizens have now abandoned those lands -: "Do you have a policy of at least bringing Uyghur Turks who are skilled in this field to these uncultivated areas and vacated villages?" Have you ever thought of such a thing?" Unfortunately, they never even thought about it.
There is no need to talk about the refugee issue anyway. In a country that does not have a population policy, nothing is being done about refugees. Refugees have gone to the most remote corners of the country, and this already appears as a big problem.

Now, the fourth problem area is informality, dear friends, but when I say "informality" here, I will not be talking about informality in the private sector. We talk a lot about informality in the private sector, but the real informality in this country is in the public sector, in the state. Now I want to tell you about them, the consequences of informality in the state are much more devastating. In other words, it evades some taxes in the private sector, provides an advantage to that company, maybe it produces a little more, exports, employs people, but the informality in the state is extremely destructive.
There is Türkiye Wealth Fund, right? 50 billion.
(Humming)
CHAIRMAN - Dear Master.
Friends, there is an extraordinarily loud buzz in the General Assembly. Please, let's maintain peace so that the orator's words can be better understood.
ERHAN USTA (Continuing) - Thank you.
Friends, the equity capital of the Türkiye Wealth Fund is 50 billion dollars, and its commercial assets are much higher. Is this Türkiye Wealth Fund within the scope of the budget? Not. Is it within the scope of audit? Not. Is it under political control? Friends, are they coming here to the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye, to this Gazi Parliament, to be informed about the Türkiye Wealth Fund? It's not done here, come on, is it done in the Planning and Budget Commission? It's not being done. unregistered,
completely off the record. TMSF, Deposit Insurance Fund. What do we know about TMSF? It has become Türkiye's largest holding company. Which number do we know? How much information do we have about the companies that came to SDIF and whether the companies in SDIF were emptied or not? Unfortunately, we are not. Major public tenders; Is there competition, is there transparency? Look, there are 10 companies in the world that receive tenders from the government, 5 of them are from Türkiye. That's why it's called "gang of 5", you know. Look, there is a tender system that always goes to certain companies. Could such a thing happen in a competitive environment? Of course not.
For example, we give the example of Osmangazi Bridge a lot. From Osmangazi Bridge, dear friends, how much does the treasury pay to the company annually? They immediately say this, they say - sir, all they know is a number - "There was a guarantee for 40 thousand vehicles to pass through here, we are providing that." My brother: "You do not guarantee vehicle passage." We don't say. For each passing vehicle, the state and the treasury pay 57 dollars to the company; more precisely, the firm receives a total of $57. Who does he get about $20 of this from? He buys from the passerby and takes 37 dollars from the treasury. We all have mobile phones and calculators; Please do a little calculation. We give 37 dollars for each passing vehicle. 40 thousand vehicles times three hundred sixty five days; What will you find? 540 million dollars, our annual treasury supplements the money of those passing through the Osmangazi Bridge, 540 million dollars. 10 billion dollars worth of work in eighteen and a half years, friends. You know you wouldn't give a penny? In whose pocket did this money go? If this money was well-deserved, we could collect it from the citizens as a normal toll. This money is not deserved money; This is the biggest corruption and biggest profiteering committed by Türkiye. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Now, another issue. There is no need to mention city hospitals.

No one in this country is aware of any contracts regarding public-private cooperation projects. I claim that, as someone who worked as general director and deputy undersecretary in the State Planning Organization for years, even we requested it, but we could not see these contracts.
Dear friends, because these contracts, these public-private cooperation agreements, are one of the main elements of corruption in Türkiye. Therefore, there are major abuses. These abuses lead to informality, informality leads to abuse, and they are in a process that feeds on each other.
Now, Law No. 5018 was passed. With this law, improvements were made in the budget appropriation system, the internal audit system was introduced, then the Court of Accounts was reformed, then the Public Procurement Law was reformed; Since I don't have time to go into details. Look, all of these are now going back one by one. A large area has begun to be created in the budget system again, an uncontrolled area. We live in an environment where there is no internal audit, the motivation of the inspection boards is lost, and the Court of Accounts is almost completely disabled. Can we talk about effectiveness in an uncontrolled environment? Of course, we will not be able to prevent corruption in an uncontrolled environment. Of course, this lack of control leads to arbitrariness, inefficiency, waste, irregularity and corruption.
Now, look, tenders. Dear friends, there is a state administration whose contract data regarding tenders is not clear and does not even compile data in a healthy way. Unfortunately, even in some units of the state, data regarding these contracts and tenders have not been compiled. Therefore, the real informality, the informality we need to focus on, is the informality in the state. When we solve this, we become able to solve many problems on our own.
Now, of course, we say that the budget is a social contract. I won't go into the details of the budget here since we've talked about it before, but let me say this: There is a budget of 19 trillion liras, 13 trillion liras of which has already been given to 4 items; personnel expenses, interest expenses, transfers to municipalities, funds and transfers to SSI. Our playing field is only 6 trillion dollars. In fact, this shows how strict the budget is and this is within it. Actually, we've been talking nonsense here for months, you know?
Currently, the government, the Strategy and Budget Presidency, and Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz have the opportunity to transfer these appropriations from one place to another as they wish - almost as they wish, 20 percent within the institution, more than 10 percent between institutions - but we think that we are making a budget here.
Now, another problem area is public administration that does not work effectively. We need strong and effective state institutions, we need strong bureaucracy. Unfortunately, those who could not distinguish between bureaucracy and red tape in this country destroyed the institutions and bureaucracy of this country by calling it "bureaucratic oligarchy". This was done on purpose, it was done voluntarily, it was premeditated, it was not something that was done randomly. The institutions of this country were knowingly and willfully collapsed. The property went.

We see the problems related to the courthouse, we have court organizations and courthouses that are used as weapons. Military in the same way, Finance in the same way, Foreign Affairs in the same way. Today, the most distinguished institutions of the state have been emptied, destroyed, and some destroyed. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) There is no State Planning Organization; Türkiye has no brain, friends. Just imagine how successful a brainless organism would be. There is no Undersecretariat of Treasury, no TODAİE, no State Personnel Presidency, no Finance Inspection Board, no Tax Inspectors Board; Unfortunately, all of these were destroyed knowingly and willfully.
We have serious problems with the public personnel system, there are problems in recruitment, problems in promotion; There is no longer any objective evaluation. Personnel rights have been weakened, plus an imbalance has begun to occur regarding personnel rights. Accountability has been forgotten among public personnel. Dear friends, the main motivation of the bureaucracy has become to serve its own pocket, not to serve the country; I say this very clearly. If so many flats are sold for 50 million lira in Ankara, it means there is a problem in this business. Moreover, in this country.
On this occasion, I would like to talk about the home ownership rate. Look, in 2002 - they always compare 2002 - the home ownership rate was 73.1 percent. What does this mean? This means a household that owns at least one house. This rate
Do you know how much it dropped in 2004? It dropped to 56 percent. The home ownership rate dropped by more than 17 points. In fact, the average number of houses per household has increased, that is, those who have 1 house now have 2, 3, 4, 5, there is an increase in the number of houses, there is an increase in the number of houses per household, but there is a decrease in the rate of home ownership. You can calculate the numbers for this. I would guess that, unfortunately, at least a significant portion of those who had a home after 2002 could not even own a single house. In an environment where there is so much corruption, income is shared so unfairly.
I will give one more example of corruption. I explained the details of this in great detail in the Planning and Budget Commission, friends who are curious can look there. EMRA. One of the most corrupt institutions in Türkiye is EPDK. Now, in 2023, EMRA issues licenses for wind and solar power plants, and in 2024, this time with a tender - EMRA does not hold a tender, but gives it without a tender - and the Ministry of Energy issues it. Look, friends, we compared the licenses given by the Ministry of Energy as a tender with the licenses given by EMRA without a tender. It gave a license for 34 thousand megawatts because EMRA. Do you know how much will be thrown at this nation and this treasury from here for twenty years? $53.1 billion, yes, $53.1 billion. Look, we need to appreciate those who do well. The difference between the tender made by the Ministry of Energy and the works awarded by EMRA without a tender will come from the treasury in this tender, therefore it will be paid out with taxes or will be charged to the public bill. Therefore, I am making a call to the President from here: The investment of these associate licenses has not started yet, the associate licenses have been distributed, this has happened as a matter of right.

Friends, the transfer fee of one 1 megawatt license is currently worth 200 thousand dollars in bag shops. All of these associate licenses should be cancelled. This is 53.1 billion dollars
It is necessary to save this nation from the burden and stake. I hereby appeal to Mr. President in this sense.
Now, this is rottenness, this is rottenness; The public is rotten, the state is rotten. What more is the corruption? Judicial relics are being stolen in this country. Sir, the population information of 86 million people is lying on the ground. Sir, a referee places a bet 18,277 times, the state is not aware of it. We asked the Minister about this: "Well, where were you, friend, until this time? One person placed a bet 18,277 times; where were you?" But unfortunately this is what happens when it is rotten.
Look, there is also the BRSA issue. Since there is a new answer, I want to share it with you here. BRSA gives permission to the company called "PayFix", which has been investigated for illegal betting, and the intermediary institution to purchase banks in 2023. When was the investigation started? What investigation? Friends, the illegal betting investigation started in 2021. In fact, the CEO and owner of this PayFix was previously tried in 2004, in 2014, and again in 2021. So now, to own a bank, you have to be so clean and white, our Banking Law requires this, but this BRSA gives permission to an institution that has an investigation to purchase a bank. Do you see the rot? Now, we asked this, the BRSA President finally had to answer; Do you know what the answer is? "I received information from the relevant institutions, but I did not receive any negative intelligence." he says. Well, we thought BDDK was rotten, the whole state is rotten my friend! (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) It is not possible to understand how such information does not come or what this is. Or it is not possible to understand whether it was an instruction from a respected elder and that is why all institutions remained silent. We are waiting for an answer to this, Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz. Look, there is a big accusation. Let me give you the article, or rather, I shared this article on my social media. BRSA says: "I asked all the institutions, but no one said anything to me." What have you done to the state? Can such a thing happen, friends? Either the BRSA is lying or there is a really serious problem in all institutions.
Now,
The Minister of National Defense - I wish he were here - points his finger at the mothers of martyrs and puts them in their place; Do you see the rot? This is the Turkish Armed Forces, no one should do it, but what if another civilian does it? Oh, the Minister of National Defense is doing this! "We are Ataturk's soldiers!" Young lieutenants who say this are dismissed from the army, but the Minister of National Defense does not say a word against those who call "murderers" and "rapists" against the identity and institutional identity of the Turkish Armed Forces in the Planning and Budget Commission. Do you see the rot? The state has become like this! (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
The data analysis capacity and policy-making capacity of institutions have become extremely shallow. I will give just one example, hundreds of examples can be given. Look - Planners are here again, former Planners - until 2016, we knew that the rate of domestic savings in Türkiye was 15 percent, friends.

Now, TurkStat made a change overnight, changed the national income series and said: A little over 25 percent; Increased it from 15 to 25. Well, it increased from 15 to 25, even now the domestic savings rate is 30, but there is no change in Türkiye's, or more precisely, the government's policy set. In other words, there is no such thing as data-based analysis or data-based policy making. What if what you talked about when domestic savings is 15 is the same as when it is 30 - which is the most basic economic indicator - can something like this happen, can such a disgrace happen? They are still strangling the citizens; "We will not give money to retirees." "We will not give money to tradesmen." they say.
Now, look, he is still holding back from the student; In 2013, a student with a one-month scholarship bought 93 chicken doner kebabs, but in 2025, this number decreased to 13 chicken doner kebabs. So you are cutting down on students, there is no need to explain the situation of retirees, you are cutting down on agriculture. Now, therefore, the country cannot be governed with dry stubbornness and black ignorance. There is no point in making citizens pay the price of wrong economic policies.
There are those who say the cost of exchange rate protected deposits is "60 billion dollars"; wrong. The two-year cost - no 2025 - is 73 billion dollars, exchange rate protected
cost of deposit. Now, also, those policies that started in September 2021. Exchange rate protected deposits were a result of that, there is a cost from this, plus there is another cost - I cannot go into details again - friends, the increase in our domestic and foreign interest liability alone is 237 billion dollars. Look, the four-year bill brought by those Nas policies is 237 billion dollars for us. With this, exactly 200 Osmangazi Bridges could have been built, so these are wrong policies. Türkiye's impoverishment is not without reason.
Now, actually, all these things happened during the AK PARTY governments period - no need for many graphics - this is the whole story, this is the whole summary: Think of this as the year 2002, this is the year 2025; Dear friends, I call this the bowl economy. Some things were bad, they were taken, they were improved, now they are worse than before. Look, put inflation here and you will see this graph; put the budget deficit and you will see this graph; Look at the income distribution and you will see this; Look at interest payments and you will see this; Look at interest rates and you will see this; Look at the idle workforce and you will see this. Yes, good work has been done, but many issues, many macroeconomic indicators and micro indicators have become worse than before. Thank God, we must congratulate them, they have put the cost of twenty-two years into a graph; They fit twenty-two and twenty-three years into a graph, they are very competent in this regard, I have to express that, dear friends. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Now, the lack of a rational and honorable foreign policy is another problem area. I will not go into much detail about this, but let me just say this: Türkiye, look, does not have a China strategy. Our imports with China have reached 50 billion dollars, our exports are only 3 billion dollars; We don't have a strategy. Uyghur Turks are being atrocities there, we cannot open our mouths and say anything to China, or what cannot we do about our relations with the technology giant Taiwan due to China's pressure and fear of China? We cannot develop. Therefore, there needs to be a China strategy.

(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Complete it, please.
I gave four minutes of additional time.
ERHAN MASTER (Continuing) - Thank you very much, it's over.
I asked the Minister of Energy: Türkiye in the Eastern Mediterranean
why not? We said it was a blue homeland, right? There is no Türkiye, Norway came to the Eastern Mediterranean last time, there is no Türkiye. Why did you buy 8 ships, brother, if we weren't going to be in the Eastern Mediterranean? Someone tell us this, we pulled all our ships from there.
Now, we asked the Minister of Foreign Affairs, we are so good, we have become a regional power, why don't you make an exclusive economic zone agreement with Syria? We need to do it first with Syria, then even with Egypt; why don't you do this? There is no sound.
SÜLEYMAN KARAMAN (Erzincan) - We did it.
ERHAN USTA (Continuing) - No, where was it done?
There is Meis Island right under our nose, right? Look, the continental shelf issue is a very important issue. Now, according to international law, the continental shelf is made on the main continents, the main lands, but according to this Seville map, Greece: "The Island of Meis is mine and I will have a continental shelf on the Island of Meis." says friends; It is 2 kilometers from us and 580 kilometers from mainland Greece, but it reveals the continental shelf here.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Complete your words.
ERHAN USTA (Continuing) - I am finishing, Mr. President.
However, there are other international examples; England and France have an arbitration decision; see same. There, according to the equidistance method based on the main continents, they found the midpoint and separated the continental shelves accordingly. There is a Yemen-Eritrea decision. Although all international court decisions are in our favor, we cannot even claim to the other party, let alone make them accept this: A continental shelf is being built through Meis Island. Therefore, of course, the result of such a foreign policy will be poverty.
I don't want to extend my words any further, thank you very much.
Therefore, I express that we, as the İYİ Party Group, will vote "no" to this exhausting budget of the government that has exhausted the nation, and I respectfully greet the General Assembly. (Applause from the İYİ Party and YENİ YOL rows)
